tool.name: sizeseq
tool.summary: Sort sequence records by size in deterministic order

sections.title: Overview
sections.content: `sizeseq` reorders a sequence stream by record length. The Epithema v1 behavior is explicit and deterministic: sort records by descending size and preserve original input order for ties.

sections.title: Inputs
sections.content: The current interface accepts one local sequence input through the shared IO path. Multi-record FASTA is the primary exercised format, while other sequence formats depend on the shared readers already used by the sequence-transform cohort.

sections.title: Outputs
sections.content: The tool emits one reordered FASTA sequence collection. CLI summaries report the source input, the descending-length stable ordering rule, and FASTA output format.

sections.title: Current Status
sections.content: This method is implemented and exposed through `epithema sizeseq`. Validation currently covers deterministic descending size ordering with stable ties against a committed four-record FASTA fixture, and Rust service tests exercise the same repository-managed case.

sections.title: Caveats
sections.content: The first release does not expose ascending sort mode or secondary sort keys beyond stable tie preservation. It is a deterministic record reordering tool, not a reporting-only size summary.

artifacts.label: Four-record varied-length FASTA fixture
artifacts.description: Repository-managed FASTA fixture containing four records with distinct and tied lengths for deterministic sizeseq validation.

examples.title: Sort a four-record FASTA fixture by descending size
examples.description: Runs `sizeseq` against a committed varied-length FASTA fixture and returns the records in descending length order while preserving tie order.

expected_outputs.label: Size-sorted sequence collection
expected_outputs.description: A FASTA sequence collection ordered as `long`, `middle`, `short`, then `short_tie`.
